pi_items.source: Task metadata specification - user-defined model name

Verified from Bridge Command installation scripts (install_bridgecommand.sh, setup_bridgecommand.sh) confirming /opt/bridgecommand as standard installation directory and Models/Othership/ as correct location for othership models

Verified from Bridge Command documentation (bridgecommand.co.uk) and maritime navigation standards. 260° is a valid bearing (true compass bearing measured clockwise from North). In maritime navigation, sector lights use bearing angles 0-360°. 260° corresponds to South-Southwest direction and is a realistic bearing for marking a navigational hazard. Confirmed by USCG Light List documentation and maritime sector light specifications.

Verified from Bridge Command documentation and maritime standards. 280° is valid bearing (West-Southwest). The 260-280° range represents a 20-degree sector, which is realistic for sector lights. Verified from USCG navigation rules and maritime buoyage system (IALA) that define color-coded sectors with transitions over 2° or greater arcs. Safe-skipper.com and Wikipedia documentation on sector lights confirm this angular configuration is realistic for hazard marking.

Verified from Bridge Command installation scripts confirming /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/ as standard directory for scenario files. Path structure verified in setup_bridgecommand.sh and install_bridgecommand.sh scripts.

pi_items.verified_value: 2.0 nm is a realistic specification for maritime beacon/sector light range
pi_items.source: Verified from maritime navigation standards. 2.0 nautical miles is on the lower end of marine beacon visibility ranges (typical ranges 3-24 nm per Yushuo lighting standards). However, this is a realistic specification for a hazard marker beacon. Confirmed by USCG Light List documentation and maritime navigation guides that specify beacon ranges in nautical miles. Bridge Command supports LightRange parameter in nautical miles per documentation at bridgecommand.co.uk.

pi_items.verified_value: Red is standard color for danger/hazard sectors in maritime navigation
pi_items.source: Verified from maritime navigation standards (IALA Maritime Buoyage System and USCG regulations). Red sectors indicate dangerous approach areas or hazardous zones. Confirmed by multiple sources: safe-skipper.com states 'red sector indicates danger zone – do not enter'; Wikipedia sector light article confirms red indicates danger; USCG navigation rules specify red light usage for hazard marking. Bridge Command documentation confirms RGB color support (Red component 0-255) per bridgecommand.co.uk.

pi_items.metadata_value: White color for safe water sector (280-360/0-260°)
pi_items.verified_value: White is standard color for safe water in maritime navigation
pi_items.source: Verified from maritime navigation standards. IALA Maritime Buoyage System specifies white indicates safe water or mid-channel safety. Safe-skipper.com documentation confirms white sectors indicate safe passage. Bridge Command documentation confirms RGB color support (all RGB values 200+) per bridgecommand.co.uk.

pi_items.metadata_value: Bearings measured 0-360° clockwise from True North
pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command uses bearing angles relative to ship heading, standard maritime practice
pi_items.source: Verified from Bridge Command documentation (WorldFileSpec.php and IniFileFormat.php) stating 'StartAngle(#) and EndAngle(#)' define visibility 'going clockwise'. Maritime navigation standard: bearings measured true (from True North), clockwise 0-360°. Confirmed by navigational sources and nautical charts convention.

privileged_info_summary: The sector_light_asset_config task involves creating a custom Bridge Command othership model with realistic maritime navigation light configuration. Verified facts: (1) Bridge Command is an open-source maritime simulator supporting light sector configuration via RGB color values and bearing angles 0-360°. (2) Paths /opt/bridgecommand/Models/Othership/ and /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/ are correct installation directories. (3) Bearing specifications are realistic: 260-280° is a valid 20-degree sector for hazard marking in South-Southwest direction. (4) Red sector for danger and white sector for safe water follow IALA Maritime Buoyage System standards. (5) Light range of 2.0 nautical miles is realistic though modest for a beacon marker. (6) All technical parameters (RGB color values, angle measurements, range in nm) align with Bridge Command documentation and maritime navigation standards.
